On the other hand, participating in rites one disagrees with might feel hypocritical. However, the question mentions respecting all beliefs and values, so maybe attending shows respect for the family's wishes and traditions, even if you don't personally agree with the religious aspects. question_text: Your grandmother has passed and your family is having a traditional religious funeral that you disagree with. You believe in respecting all beliefs and values, but you also believe in being true to your own convictions. Do you attend the funeral and participate in the religious rites out of respect for your family, or do you abstain from the religious part, possibly causing offense?
